title: Messaging Outcomes, Delivery Claims, and Evidence · Compiler Case: Delivery Guarantee Claim
summary: A team calls every background task reliable pub/sub, promises exactly-once delivery, and reports publish calls instead of stakeholder, ownership, loss, duplicate, latency, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the delivery guarantee claim increment in messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: State at-most-once, at-least-once, effectively-once effect, ordering, and durability claims without promising impossible end-to-end exactly-once delivery.
Distinguish commands, events, notifications, documents, and queries by ownership, audience, expected handling, and change semantics.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Delivery Guarantee Claim · Theory Delivery Guarantee Claim
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 9656 in a high-volume telemetry route with one hot customer key. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to separate local publish, broker ownership, and observable stakeholder effect; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then lower the byte or in-flight budget and preserve upstream rejection evidence. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Delivery Guarantee Claim contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Delivery Guarantee Claim · Theory Delivery Guarantee Claim.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Delivery Guarantee Claim · Theory Delivery Guarantee Claim, keep Delivery Guarantee Claim connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name publisher intent, exchange and routing evidence, queue responsibility, message identity, ownership transfer, byte, time, attempt, and concurrency budgets, terminal delivery decision, effect, cleanup, and recovery; run a pure deterministic TypeScript model; then change one message or failure and state the amqplib, RabbitMQ, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, or production transfer gate. Apply that move specifically to Delivery Guarantee Claim while producing messaging outcome, delivery claim, and evidence charter.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Build Delivery Guarantee Claim evidence · Theory Delivery Guarantee Claim
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: rabbit-ts-delivery-guarantee-claim-zghth1" that uses case 1725 to separate local publish, broker ownership, and observable stakeholder effect. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not import amqplib or Node built-ins, open sockets, contact RabbitMQ, Docker, networks, databases, or external effects, read host state, or use credentials; verify those boundaries later with the named Node 24, amqplib 2.0.1, RabbitMQ 4.3, TLS, partition, restart, load, upgrade, and production transfer gates.
